title: olearia sarawaketensis
intro: Olearia sarawaketensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to New Guinea. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Olearia sarawaketensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable medicinal plant in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Olearia sarawaketensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
